entries.mem0ai/mem0: As the official memory provider for Hermes Agent, Mem0 offers a universal layer that combines semantic, keyword, and entity-based matching for multi-signal retrieval. It utilizes temporal reasoning to rank memories by relevance and features a single-pass extraction process that accumulates facts without overwriting existing data.
entries.garrytan/gbrain: This opinionated memory system features a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entity relationships without requiring LLM calls. It employs a hybrid search strategy that boosts vector retrieval with backlink-based graph ranking and supports 14 different embedding providers.
entries.vectorize-io/hindsight: Hindsight implements a 'retain, recall, and reflect' workflow designed for long-term agent learning and enterprise-grade storage via PostgreSQL or Oracle. It simplifies memory management through a two-line LLM wrapper that automates the persistence of agent experiences.
entries.plastic-labs/honcho: Honcho is a sophisticated library that uses asynchronous reasoning to extract conclusions from message traces and event logs. Its peer-centric model tracks evolving entities and multi-peer perspectives, utilizing hybrid BM25 and vector search for nuanced context retrieval.
entries.greyhaven-ai/autocontext: This recursive harness uses a specialized five-agent loop to iteratively improve context for complex tasks. It generates structured playbooks and traces to ensure knowledge persistence across sessions for Hermes and other LLM providers.
entries.stephenschoettler/hermes-lcm: A lossless context management plugin that uses a hierarchical DAG structure to ensure no message is ever truly lost during context compaction. It provides agents with specialized tools to search, describe, and expand compressed history stored in a persistent SQLite backend.
entries.elkimek/honcho-self-hosted: This project provides a self-hosted backend for the Honcho memory library, enabling private cross-session persistence for Hermes Agent. It includes automated Docker setup scripts and supports any OpenAI-compatible provider or local inference server.
entries.yoloshii/ClawMem: ClawMem is a local-first context engine that operates entirely without cloud dependencies or API keys. It integrates via MCP or agent plugins, utilizing a hybrid retrieval stack of BM25, vector search, and knowledge graphs.
entries.EfficientContext/ContextPilot: Focused on performance, this tool optimizes long-context inference by reducing prompt tokens by up to 36% through deduplication. It significantly boosts prefill speeds for Hermes Agent and vLLM by utilizing optimized prefix sharing.
entries.plur-ai/plur: Plur introduces a shared memory layer using an open YAML 'engram' format to facilitate zero-cost local storage. It implements ACT-R activation logic to strengthen relevant facts while allowing stale information to decay naturally over time.
entries.keepnotes-ai/keep: Keep provides reflective memory by indexing codebases, documents, and media with automatic OCR and summarization. It creates navigable, bidirectional relationships between notes using edge tags, accessible via MCP and CLI interfaces.
entries.AxDSan/mnemosyne: A zero-dependency memory system designed for sub-millisecond latency in read, write, and search operations. It functions as a native Hermes plugin, leveraging SQLite for local-first storage with integrated vector and full-text search.
entries.yepyhun/Brainstack: Brainstack consolidates temporal continuity, graph truth, and corpus retrieval into a single kernel stack for Hermes. It employs a unique 'five-shelf' architecture to organize data and prevent the common issue of flat, unorganized memory blobs.
entries.amanning3390/flowstate-qmd: This anticipatory memory system prefetches context by monitoring agent session logs to stay ahead of user needs. Built for the Hermes 2026 Hackathon, it uses SQLite-vec and GGUF models for a completely local-first, MCP-native experience.

entries.mukul975/Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills: This project provides 754 structured cybersecurity skills mapped to major industry frameworks like MITRE ATT&CK and NIST. It uses the standardized agentskills.io format to enable sub-second discovery and execution across 26 specialized security domains.
entries.AMAP-ML/SkillClaw: SkillClaw automates the deduplication and quality improvement of agent skills across different devices and frameworks. It enables collective evolution by allowing user groups to share and refine learned skills within a unified library.
entries.conorbronsdon/avoid-ai-writing: This auditing tool uses a 109-entry replacement table and a two-pass audit system to eliminate detectable AI writing patterns. It identifies 42 distinct markers, such as significance inflation and copula avoidance, to ensure content reads naturally.
entries.wondelai/skills: This is a cross-platform library compatible with Claude Code and agentskills.io-compliant agents. It offers modular installation of specialized frameworks for architecture, UX design, marketing, and product strategy via a dedicated CLI.
entries.DougTrajano/pydantic-ai-skills: This implementation of the Agent Skills open specification is designed specifically for Pydantic AI. It utilizes progressive disclosure of metadata to reduce token usage while supporting both programmatic and filesystem-based skill definitions.
entries.Agents365-ai/drawio-skill: This skill enables agents to generate complex draw.io diagrams from natural language descriptions, supporting formats like XML, PNG, and SVG. It features a self-check feedback loop to automatically fix layout issues across various diagram types including UML and ERDs.
entries.Cranot/super-hermes: Super-hermes teaches agents to write their own analytical prompts and custom thinking instructions for complex problem-solving. It helps identify structural trade-offs in codebases and generates reports to highlight analysis blind spots.
entries.smartcontractkit/chainlink-agent-skills: This toolkit provides standardized skills for interacting with Chainlink oracle networks, including CCIP and Data Feeds. It includes CLI tools for local simulations and monitoring cross-chain smart contract workflows.
entries.tlehman/litprog-skill: This literate programming skill allows agents to weave source code, prose, and diagrams into unified .lit.md files. It includes a tangler to extract runnable files and automatically syncs edits back to the primary documentation.
entries.esaradev/icarus-plugin: Icarus facilitates cross-instance shared memory and the creation of replacement models through markdown-based directories. It features an end-to-end pipeline for extracting decision-quality training data to fine-tune and evaluate agent successors.
entries.chigwell/skilldock.io: Skilldock.io is a registry for reusable AI skills that features recursive dependency resolution and an OpenAPI-driven SDK. It integrates a commerce flow supporting Stripe and TON blockchain for the management and discovery of agent capabilities.
entries.black-forest-labs/skills: These are the official image generation skills for the FLUX model family, providing standardized prompting guidelines and API integration patterns. They support precise JSON-structured prompting and hex color specifications for high-fidelity visual outputs.
entries.Romanescu11/hermes-skill-factory: This meta-skill plugin monitors active workflows to automatically detect and propose new, reusable skills. It generates both Python scaffolds and documentation, allowing users to save and manage detected patterns via slash commands.
entries.ReinaMacCredy/maestro: Maestro is a harness for long-running agents that utilizes a local-first Git-based state management system. It follows a structured 'spec-task-verify-ship' lifecycle and uses passive handoff envelopes for seamless context transfer between agents.
entries.tiann/execplan-skill: This skill enables autonomous execution of multi-step tasks with built-in checkpoints and recovery procedures. It tracks progress in real-time and provides rollback capabilities to ensure safe execution of complex, long-running operations.
entries.cablate/Agentic-MCP-Skill: This progressive MCP client uses a three-layer lazy loading system to minimize token consumption when handling large toolsets. It maintains persistent connections to multiple MCP servers via a socket-based daemon for efficient tool inspection and retrieval.
entries.armelhbobdad/bmad-module-skill-forge: Skill Forge converts existing repositories and developer discourse into version-pinned, agentskills.io-compliant instructions. It provides verifiable audit trails with line-level citations to ensure the accuracy of generated agent skills.
entries.amanning3390/hermeshub: HermesHub is a community marketplace for browsing and installing skills, featuring automated security scanning with over 65 threat detection rules. It includes an agent-to-agent feedback system and supports both crypto and Stripe-based payments for creators.
entries.PederHP/skillsdotnet: This C# and .NET implementation of the agentskills.io specification integrates with Microsoft.Extensions.AI for tool-based loading. It supports progressive disclosure via SKILL.md files and provides automatic registration for common skill directories.
entries.adnw-vinc/hermes-nextcloud: This skill allows Hermes to manage Nextcloud files, calendars, and contacts using standard WebDAV and CalDAV protocols. It ensures secure communication via App Passwords and includes a guided setup script for easy configuration.

entries.numtide/llm-agents.nix: This project provides a collection of reproducible Nix packages for various AI coding tools, including the Hermes Agent. It is ideal for users who require daily automated updates and a consistent environment across different agent platforms like Claude Code and Goose.
entries.TheAiSingularity/hermesclaw: Designed for high-security environments, this project implements hardware-enforced sandboxing using NVIDIA OpenShell and kernel-level security layers. It features dynamic policy presets to strictly control network and filesystem access while supporting over 40 built-in skills for automated workflows.
entries.0xrsydn/nix-hermes-agent: This repository offers a NixOS module for declarative management of Hermes deployments, including systemd services and secure secrets handling via sops-nix. It bridges the gap between managed infrastructure and flexibility by supporting both Nix-defined and interactive CLI skill installations.
entries.rookiemann/portable-hermes-agent: This zero-install Windows solution provides a GUI-based agent equipped with over 100 tools and local model support via LM Studio. It is the premier choice for desktop users seeking a portable, Docker-free environment that includes a dynamic tool maker and workflow engine.
entries.xmbshwll/hermes-agent-docker: This project provides a streamlined Docker image that bundles the official Hermes installation with mini-swe-agent for a ready-to-use sandbox. It simplifies deployment by supporting persistent state through mounted directories and allowing users to target specific Hermes versions via build arguments.
entries.jpalmae/hermeshq: A Docker-first control plane, this application orchestrates multiple Hermes instances with distinct identities and separate workspaces. It is unique for its focus on inter-agent delegation, task scheduling, and providing enterprise-grade MCP access for external AI clients.
entries.JackTheGit/hermes-autonomous-server: Focused on headless production environments, this project configures the Hermes Gateway as a reboot-safe systemd service on Ubuntu and Debian. It is specifically optimized for running autonomous, recurring AI cron jobs without the need for a persistent user session.
entries.ellickjohnson/portainer-stack-hermes: This deployment stack integrates Hermes with a ttyd web terminal, allowing users to interact with the agent directly through a browser. It is designed for easy management via Portainer and utilizes GitHub Actions for automated image builds.

entries.builderz-labs/mission-control: This self-hosted orchestration platform manages multi-agent workflows across frameworks like CrewAI and LangGraph using a zero-dependency SQLite backend. It is essential for Hermes users who require cost monitoring and a human-in-the-loop 'Aegis' system for manual task sign-off.
entries.swarmclawai/swarmclaw: Swarmclaw enables the creation of autonomous agent swarms with hierarchical delegation and durable memory across more than 23 model providers. It stands out by allowing Hermes-based agents to learn new skills directly from conversation history while maintaining complex organizational structures.
entries.howdymary/hermes-agent-metaharness: This specialized framework utilizes an automated outer-loop search process to optimize the harness code used for evaluating Hermes Agent performance. It orchestrates TBLite and TB2 benchmarks, tracking performance frontiers through structured candidate mutation and comparison.
entries.runtimenoteslabs/gladiator: Gladiator creates a competitive arena where two autonomous AI companies, powered by Hermes Agent and Paperclip, vie for GitHub stars. The system orchestrates nine distinct agents and provides a real-time dashboard to visualize task execution, code generation, and scoreboard metrics.
entries.1ilkhamov/opencode-hermes-multiagent: This project coordinates 17 specialized agents across six functional domains to handle complex software engineering tasks like research, planning, and infrastructure. It enforces a strict pipeline architecture with mandatory security audits and quality gates for every code change.
entries.supermodeltools/bigiron: Bigiron implements an AI-native software development lifecycle by providing Hermes with structural code graphs via the Supermodel MCP. It executes an autonomous eight-phase process that uses graph-diff phase gates to ensure architectural integrity from planning to final health checks.
entries.Rainhoole/hermes-agent-acp-skill: This delegation framework routes tasks between Hermes subagents, Codex, and Claude Code while preventing parent context pollution through isolated workflows. It prioritizes operational safety by enforcing configurable timeouts and strict output character limits on all delegated actions.

entries.junhoyeo/tokscale: This Rust-based TUI provides local data visualization for tracking token consumption and costs across multiple AI coding environments. It is essential for Hermes developers who need to monitor resource usage alongside other agents like Claude Code and Gemini.
entries.joeynyc/hermes-skins: This utility allows users to personalize the Hermes terminal UI through YAML-based configuration files. It supports custom ASCII banners and spinner animations, offering a way to create and share unique visual themes while inheriting default system behaviors.
entries.AlexAI-MCP/hermes-CCC: This project ports the Hermes Agent brain and 46 core skills directly into the Claude Code Channel for a native, process-free experience. It eliminates the need for OAuth and external processes while adding specialized MLOps tools for vLLM and RL training.
entries.unmodeled-tyler/vessel-browser: An AI-native browser designed for autonomous control, this tool exposes web navigation to agents via the Model Context Protocol (MCP). It features a supervisory UI with annotated checkpoints, allowing developers to audit and supervise Hermes Agent as it performs browser-based tasks.
entries.Abruptive/Ankh.md: This framework enables the creation of scoped Hermes Agents that are restricted to specific project folders. It allows for seamless switching between default and local agents, each with its own project-specific memories, tools, and custom instructions.
entries.roli-lpci/lintlang: A static linter that performs deterministic scans of agent configurations and system prompts without incurring LLM costs. It helps developers maintain high-quality Hermes implementations by detecting seven specific structural failure modes, including H1-H7 patterns.
entries.0xNyk/openclaw-to-hermes: This migration utility automates the transition from OpenClaw to Hermes Agent by mapping personas, memories, and skills to the Hermes YAML format. It ensures a smooth onboarding process by handling the transfer of complex model configurations and local credentials.
entries.42-evey/evey-setup: This Docker-based deployment stack automates the setup of Hermes Agent with LiteLLM, Ollama, and Qdrant for local GPU inference. It comes pre-configured with 29 community plugins for memory and social integration, enabling a zero-cost agent environment in minutes.

entries.nesquena/hermes-webui: A versatile web and mobile interface featuring a three-panel layout for chat, navigation, and file browsing. It provides full parity with the Hermes CLI and includes a visual context ring to monitor token usage in real-time.
entries.EKKOLearnAI/hermes-web-ui: A comprehensive dashboard designed for multi-platform deployment across eight messaging channels, including WhatsApp and WeChat. It offers robust session management, scheduled jobs, and detailed usage analytics for tracking costs and model distribution.
entries.outsourc-e/hermes-workspace: A native web workspace that unifies chat, terminal operations, and memory browsing into a single dashboard. It features Swarm Mode for role-based dispatch of multiple persistent agents and integrates seamlessly with vanilla Hermes installations.
entries.xaspx/hermes-control-interface: A self-hosted gateway for managing the Hermes AI agent stack through a browser-based terminal and file editor. It provides granular control over individual agent profiles alongside real-time system metrics and token analytics.
entries.dodo-reach/hermes-desktop: A native macOS application that connects directly to remote Hermes hosts via SSH without requiring middleman APIs. The workspace includes integrated Kanban boards, remote file management, and local workflow presets.
entries.clawvader-tech/hermes-telegram-miniapp: A mobile-first React SPA designed specifically for the Telegram Mini App ecosystem. It supports background agent spawning for up to five concurrent instances and features terminal-style chat with real-time system monitoring.
entries.aivrar/portable-hermes-agent: A zero-install Windows desktop GUI that requires no Docker or admin rights to run. It includes over 100 integrated tools—such as ComfyUI and TTS—and features a dynamic tool maker for creating API wrappers at runtime.
entries.fathah/hermes-desktop: A dedicated desktop companion app that simplifies the installation and configuration of local or remote Hermes backends. It manages 14 different toolsets and provides a gateway for connecting to platforms like Discord and Slack.
entries.sanchomuzax/hermes-webui: A specialized dashboard focused on process monitoring and service health. Users can perform full-text searches of conversation histories and manage scheduled agent cron jobs and skill sources.
entries.Euraika-Labs/pan-ui: A chat-centric workspace that features an integrated marketplace for discovering skills and MCP servers. It emphasizes security through isolated profile management and real-time tool approval cards.
entries.pyrate-llama/hermes-ui: A lightweight, glassmorphic web interface built on a single-file React architecture. It streamlines workspace management by providing inline file previews and integrated controls for MCP tools, skills, and cron jobs.
